summary: rc.20 Cite Policy — close the loop on observable KB usage. AI replies must lead with `KB: <id> (<≤8字>) [tag]` or `KB: none` before edit/decide/plan; `fab doctor --cite-coverage` produces bilingual coverage report computed from events.jsonl replay joined against agents.meta.json relevance_paths denominator. Scope: Claude Code first-class + Codex assume-and-test; Cursor deferred to rc.21 (PreToolUse cannot see assistant text).
approach: 11-commit linear chain implementing 4 vertical slices: (1) Protocol contract — extend BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL with `## Cite policy` H2, propagating to all three end blocks via existing fab install managed-block writer; (2) Data plane — register assistant_turn_observed + cite_policy_activated event variants in shared Zod discriminated union; extend fabric-hint.cjs summarizeTranscript to capture first-line `KB:` per assistant envelope + emit one event per turn (hook-side inline JSON.stringify, node_modules unavailable); (3) Observability plane — `fab doctor --cite-coverage` flag with --since=7d / --client=cc|codex|cursor|all plumbed through CLI; fast-path skips 28-check pipeline; idempotent cite_policy_activated marker emit on first invocation; single-pass events.jsonl replay computing edits_touched / qualifying_cites / recalled_unverified / expected_but_missed / total_turns metrics joined against minimatch glob denominator; bilingual report formatter honoring locked 中文 metric names (Edit 触达数 / 合格 cite / 应查没查); (4) Quality plane — comprehensive server-side cite-coverage tests (14 cases including 10k-event perf <100ms) + hook-side capture tests (parseKbLine all enum + Zod-roundtrip + never-throws); self-host refresh of three-end blocks; release bump to 2.0.0-rc.20.

design_decisions.rationale: MEMORY locked schema: one assistant_turn_observed event per turn carries kb_line_raw + cite_ids[] + cite_tags[]; replay derives planned/recalled/chained-from/dismissed buckets from cite_tags enum. Reduces ledger noise; matches per-turn semantic boundary.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Replay must split tag enum into buckets at read-time vs at write-time; trivial CPU cost, cleaner data model.

design_decisions.decision: Marker emit at first `fab doctor --cite-coverage`, NOT at install
design_decisions.rationale: Idempotent check-before-emit; lazy creation = clean test fixture (empty events.jsonl is valid initial state); users who never invoke --cite-coverage incur zero pollution.
design_decisions.tradeoff: First invocation always shows 0% historical coverage (marker_ts = now) — communicated via marker_emitted_now warning in report header.

design_decisions.rationale: Matches doctor's existing flat flag-only design; --cite-coverage suppresses normal 28-check pipeline → fast-path. Locked by auto-orchestrator Q-3 option 3.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Mutex with --fix/--fix-knowledge must be enforced explicitly; small validation cost.

design_decisions.decision: Turn boundary = `${session_id}-${envelope_index}` as turn_id; first non-empty text line of first text block
design_decisions.rationale: Stable, deterministic, no UUID dependency. Claude Code transcript JSONL pre-separates turns one-per-envelope; envelope_index gives unique per-session id.
design_decisions.tradeoff: If assistant emits tool_use BEFORE text, the cite must still appear at first text block — model behavior assumption documented in canonical policy text.

design_decisions.rationale: Cursor PreToolUse hook sees tool_input (file paths), NOT assistant reply text; Stop hook stdin contract for Cursor is undocumented. Honest scope cut avoids shipping silent-skip pretending to work.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Two-of-three client coverage at rc.20 ship; --client=cursor returns 'not yet supported' warning.

design_decisions.decision: Single readEventLedger pass for all event_type branches (no double-read)
design_decisions.rationale: readEventLedger reads full file into memory; multiple passes = duplicate I/O. Per coverage-algo constraint #1.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Replay loop has wider switch statement; manageable since all branches are simple accumulators.

design_decisions.decision: Per-task commit style (11 commits, one per TASK)
design_decisions.rationale: User explicit preference (rc.17 RC pattern); each TASK = atomic, reviewable, revertable commit; clean chain.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Slightly higher orchestration overhead vs single mega-commit; well worth it for review surface.

data_flow.diagram: AI emits `KB: <id> [tag]` first line → Claude Code/Codex transcript JSONL → fabric-hint.cjs (Stop hook) summarizeTranscript extracts assistant envelope text → parseKbLine splits cite_ids + cite_tags → extractAndWriteAssistantTurnsBestEffort appends assistant_turn_observed to .fabric/events.jsonl → `fab doctor --cite-coverage` reads events.jsonl via readEventLedger (filtered by ts >= cite_policy_activated.ts AND ts >= since_ts) → single-pass replay joins assistant_turn_observed + edit_intent_checked + knowledge_sections_fetched → join against agents.meta.json.nodes.{stable_id}.description.relevance_paths via minimatch → compute 5 metrics + per-client split + dismissed-reason histogram → bilingual report (en/zh-CN) rendered via writeIssueSection

stages.input: Claude Code or Codex transcript JSONL (path provided via Stop hook stdin payload)
transformations: JSONL parse per line
Filter envelopes by role:'assistant'
Extract first text block first non-empty line
Apply regex /^KB:\s+(.+)$|^KB:\s*none$/
parseKbLine → {cite_ids, cite_tags}
Detect client via FABRIC_HINT_CLIENT env or path heuristic
Build event payload + inline JSON.stringify + fs.appendFileSync
readEventLedger filter by event_type:'cite_policy_activated'
If empty: appendEventLedgerEvent with policy_version + timestamp
Best-effort: read/write failure returns {marker_ts:0, emitted_now:false}

stages.output: In-memory CiteCoverageReport with 5 metrics + per_client + dismissed_reason_histogram
transformations: Single readEventLedger pass
Switch on event_type → partition into assistantTurns[] + editEvents[]
Filter by client when --client !== 'all'
Load kbIndex from agents.meta.json.nodes
For each turn: categorize cite_tags → bucket counts
For each cite_id: compute denominator via minimatch glob (narrow) or total edit count (broad)
Cross-reference recalled tags with knowledge_sections_fetched events in same session within ±60s
Compute expected_but_missed: edit on path matching kb.relevance_paths with no corresponding cite emitted in same session
Aggregate per_client by grouping turns by client field

stages.output: Stdout text (bilingual via i18n) OR JSON (--json mode)
transformations: jsonMode → JSON.stringify passthrough
Else: writeIssueSection-style block with i18n labels (en or zh-CN per LANG)
Conditional per-client + dismissed-reasons subsections
marker_emitted_now warning prepend on first run
status:'skipped' fallback when marker_ts==0

_metadata.scope_notes: Claude Code first-class + Codex assume-and-test; Cursor deferred to rc.21 (PreToolUse cannot see assistant text)
